title: Pelvic inflammatory disease - NHS
text:
til:
you and your sexual partners have finished any antibiotics you no longer have symptoms your test results show the infection has gone If you do have sex, you should use a condom .
Complications of pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) 
Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) can cause complications if it's not treated early.
These include:
lumps or swellings filled with pus (abscesses) in your fallopian tubes or ovaries long-term pelvic pain problems with your fertility a higher risk of having an ectopic pregnancy , where your baby grows outside your womb You can also keep getting pelvic inflammatory disease.

Next review due: 17 December 2028
